---
title: "firth logistic regression for species specific biomarkers"
author: "Nick Bartelo"
date: "2024-09-26"
output: html_document
---

First we install some packages.

```{r, warning=FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Install package for firth logistic regression
#install.packages("logistf")  # Install if you haven't already
library(logistf)              # Load the logistf library
library(dplyr)
```

Below we import the data and run firth logistic regression.

```{r}
# Import data
annotated_mz_data <- read.csv("annotated_mz_only_post_processed_maplet_data.csv", row.names = 1)

# Ensure Group is a factor
annotated_mz_data$Group <- as.factor(annotated_mz_data$Group)

# Initialize an empty list to store significant results
significant_results_list <- list()

# Initialize an empty list to store all results
all_results_list <- list()

# Get the list of continuous variables (excluding Group and Organism)
continuous_vars <- names(annotated_mz_data)[!(names(annotated_mz_data) %in% c("Group", "Organism", "date_measured", "batch_measured"))]

# Initialize a counter for warnings
warning_count <- 0

# Loop over each organism
for (organism in unique(annotated_mz_data$Organism)) {
  
  # Subset data for the current organism
  subset_data <- annotated_mz_data[annotated_mz_data$Organism == organism, ]
  
  # Initialize a vector to store p-values for the current organism
  pvalues <- numeric(length(continuous_vars))
  
  # Flag to check if a warning occurred for the current organism
  warning_occurred <- FALSE
  
  # Loop over each continuous variable
  for (i in seq_along(continuous_vars)) {
    metabolite <- continuous_vars[i]
    
    # Fit the Firth logistic regression model using logistf()
    model <- suppressWarnings(logistf(as.formula(paste("Group ~", metabolite)), 
                                      data = subset_data,
                                      plcontrol = logistf.control(maxit = 100)))  # Increase iterations
    
    # Check for warnings and set the flag
    if (length(warnings()) > 0) {
      warning_occurred <- TRUE  # Set the flag if any warnings are present
    }
    
    # Extract the p-value for the metabolite directly
    pvalues[i] <- summary(model)$prob[2]  # Extract p-value for the metabolite
  }
  
  # If any warnings occurred, increment the warning count
  if (warning_occurred) {
    warning_count <- warning_count + 1
  }
  
  # Store the results in a dataframe
  results <- data.frame(
    organism = organism,
    metabolite = continuous_vars,
    p_value = pvalues
  )
  
  # Apply FDR correction within this organism
  results$fdr_adjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(results$p_value, method = "fdr")

  # Filter for significant results (FDR-adjusted p-value < 0.05)
  significant_results <- results %>%
    filter(fdr_adjusted_p_value < 0.05) %>%
    select(organism, metabolite, fdr_adjusted_p_value)
  
  # Append significant results to the list
  all_results_list[[organism]] <- results
  
  # Append significant results to the list
  significant_results_list[[organism]] <- significant_results
}

# Combine all significant results into a single dataframe
all_significant_results <- bind_rows(significant_results_list)

# Combine all results into a single dataframe
all_results <- bind_rows(all_results_list)

```

Below we save the results.

```{r}
# Remove first character from all values in the metabolite column
all_results$metabolite <- substr(all_results$metabolite, 2, nchar(all_results$metabolite))

# Sort data by Organism and by FDR-adjusted p-value
all_results_sorted <- all_results %>%
  arrange(organism, fdr_adjusted_p_value)

# Save all results
write.csv(all_results_sorted, "C:/PhD rotation 1/New Analysis Annotated mz/Final Figures/firth_logistic_regression_results_all_organisms.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Remove Escherichia coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae rows from the organism column
all_results_final <- all_results %>%
  filter(organism != "Escherichia coli" & organism != "Klebsiella pneumoniae")

# Sort data by Organism and by FDR-adjusted p-value
all_results_final_sorted <- all_results_final %>%
  arrange(organism, fdr_adjusted_p_value)

# Save the final results to a CSV file
write.csv(all_results_final_sorted, "C:/PhD rotation 1/New Analysis Annotated mz/Final Figures/firth_logistic_regression_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
